mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with t-statistic > 2.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of observations, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that erode profit margins, causing negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation exceeding 8% year-over-year forces aggressive central bank policy expectations, causing immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate expanding residential investment and construction activity, which correlates with positive near-term equity returns through economic momentum.
failure_condition: HOUST increases yield negative 20-day forward returns in > 60% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that trigger immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered decline months, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample tests.
